'''Paul Johnson''' (born 13 March 1988) is an English professional [[rugby league]] footballer. He previously played for the [[Widnes Vikings]] in the [[Super League]]. Signing for [[St Helens R.F.C.|St. Helens]] from amateur side Blackbrook ARLFC, Johnson plays as either a {{rlp|pr}} or {{rlp|sr}}. He has international honours at amateur level, having featured for Great Britain Community Lions.<ref name="Paul Johnson St Helens Profile">{{cite web|url=http://www.saintsrlfc.com/player/49|title=Paul Johnson St Helens Profile|website=Saintsrlfc.com|date=2009-07-30|access-date=2009-07-30}}</ref> He was named in his first 19-man squad ahead of the derby with [[Wigan Warriors]] on Friday 31 July 2009.<ref name="St Helens vs. Wigan Preview">{{cite web|url=http://www.superleague.co.uk/preview.php?id=3783|title=St Helens vs. Wigan Preview|website=Superleague.com|date=2009-07-30|access-date=2009-07-30}}</ref> He also made the 19 for the 21 August 2009 game against the [[Huddersfield Giants]] but again dropped out.<ref name="St Helens vs. Huddersfield Preview">{{cite web|url=http://www.superleague.co.uk/preview.php?id=3796|title=St Helens vs. Huddersfield Preview|website=Superleague.com|date=2009-07-30|access-date=2009-07-30}}</ref>

==Début season (2010)==
Johnson made his first grade début for St Helens in 2010's [[Super League XV]], coming off the [[Rugby league positions#Substitutes|bench]] to play {{rlp|sr}} in a 30–22 win at home to the Huddersfield Giants.<ref name="Youngsters Come of Age in Giants Win">{{cite web|url=https://saintsrlfc.com/news/2591|title=Youngsters Come of Age in Giants Win|website=Saintsrlfc.com|date=2010-06-11|access-date=2010-06-12}}</ref>

==Move to Wakefield Trinity Wildcats==
It was announced that Johnson's first year with Saints would be his last, as he secured a two-year contract at [[Wakefield Trinity|Wakefield Trinity Wildcats]]. He has been allocated the shirt number 17. Johnson made his début in the Festive Trophy on New Years Day, when Wakefield Trinity Wildcats lost 22–40 to Leeds Rhinos. He was awarded the Man of The Match Award in this game, for his excellent contribution going forward.

==Widnes Vikings==
On 7 October 2013, it was announced that Johnson would be signing for the [[Widnes Vikings]] on a one-year contract after securing an early release from [[Hull F.C.|Hull FC]]. On 22 April 2014, Johnson extended his Widnes contract to the end of 2016.<ref>{{Cite web |url=http://www.superleague.co.uk/article/30034/johnson-extends-widnes-deal |title=Johnson extends Widnes deal |access-date=22 April 2014 |archive-date=12 February 2015 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20150212222039/http://www.superleague.co.uk/article/30034/johnson-extends-widnes-deal |url-status=dead }}</ref><ref>{{Cite news|url=https://www.bbc.com/sport/0/rugby-league/27114529|title = Prop Johnson extends Widnes deal|publisher = BBC Sport}}</ref> On 11 February 2015 Johnson and fellow Vikings teammate [[Grant Gore]] was loaned to [[Whitehaven R.L.F.C.|Whitehaven]] on a one-month deal.<ref>{{Cite web |url=http://superleague.co.uk/article/31617/widnes-duo-make-loan-move |title=Widnes duo make loan move |access-date=12 February 2015 |archive-date=12 February 2015 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20150212211714/http://superleague.co.uk/article/31617/widnes-duo-make-loan-move |url-status=dead }}</ref>
